Empires & Puzzles Talésie Hero Review: The Sanguine Assassin

Talésie brings a unique mechanic to the 4-star arena: punishment for those marked by the Blood Lily. With fast mana and rogue stats, can this Sanguine assassin bleed out the competition, or is he just a bat out of hell with nowhere to go? We dive into the numbers and strategy.
Talésie Hero Review: The Bat-Eared Assassin of Sanguine Feast
Talésie, The Sanguine Assassin: Blood, Bleed, and Bat-Like Fury

Talésie is a 4-star Epic hero from the Sanguine Feast event, introduced in late 2025. As a Dark (Purple) Rogue, he fits into the fast-paced, high-damage archetype, but with a significant twist tied to his family mechanic. He isn't your standard sniper; he is a conditional mass murderer. If you like heroes that require a bit of setup for a massive payoff, Talésie might just be your new favorite toy. If you prefer point-and-click simplicity, well, he might drive you batty.

Talésie sports a distinctively vampiric, bat-humanoid aesthetic. With large ears, a hooded cowl, and a jagged dagger glowing with ominous energy, he looks like he stumbled out of a gothic horror novel—or perhaps a heavy metal album cover. The color palette is a mix of deep purples and spectral cyans, fitting his Dark element perfectly. Unlike the noble knights or savage barbarians, Talésie looks like the creature that watches you from the gargoyles before swooping down. It's a design that screams "I'm going to bite you," which is fair warning, really.

Talésie enters the arena with very modern stats for a 4-star hero. With an attack stat hovering near 800 un-emblemed (and pushing significantly higher with emblems and Limit Breaks), he is built to hurt. His defense and health are respectable, ensuring he isn't made of wet tissue paper, but his primary stat distribution clearly favors offense. Compared to classic 4-stars or even those from a year or two ago, Talésie is a statistical powerhouse. He stands shoulder-to-shoulder with the likes of the Toon costumes and recent Construct heroes in terms of raw power.
Ravening Blade is a skill of two halves.
Deals 220% damage to a single target at Fast speed. On its own, this is underwhelming. Standard 4-star snipers usually hit for 300-400%. If this was all he did, he'd be food.
Deals 180% damage AND 189 Bleed damage over 3 turns to
all enemies marked with a Blood Lily

This is where the magic happens. The Blood Lily is applied automatically by his Family Bonus (and other family members). This means his special skill scales drastically as the battle progresses and more enemies get marked. He transforms from a weak sniper into a Fast mana AoE nuker who also applies a DoT. The synergy is internal, but it requires time or teammates to fully ramp up.
Fast. He needs 8 tiles to charge, or 7 with a level 17 mana troop (or other bonuses). This allows him to fire frequently, which is essential for applying pressure.
63 damage per turn for 3 turns (189 total) per marked enemy.
If 3 enemies are marked, his total damage output jumps from a measly 220% single target to
. That is a massive swing in total damage output.
Don't forget the Sanguine Mark passive. Damage is increased by 5% per Blood Lily stack. If a target has 5 stacks, he deals 25% more damage to them.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Fleur de Sang family brings a mechanic that defines Talésie's existence.

turn, the character marks the enemy with the highest HP. This is automatic. This means even if you run Talésie alone, he will start marking targets. By the time you charge him (likely turn 3 or 4), there will be 3 or 4 marks distributed among the enemy team (or stacked on one). This stack deals damage over time and heals the Fleur de Sang family. It is the engine that powers his special skill.
This is a nice damage multiplier. Since the Blood Lily stacks (up to 10), this passive ensures that as the fight drags on, Talésie hits harder and harder against the most resilient enemies.

Attack Up is the perfect Aether Power for him. It boosts his base attack for the first 6 turns, which synergizes with his Fast mana speed, allowing his first strike (even if only one or two enemies are marked) to sting significantly more.
Talésie is part of the "Sanguine Feast" wave, introducing the Blood Lily mechanic. He represents a shift towards heroes that create their own win conditions over time rather than just upfront burst.

His stats are top-tier for 4-stars. While his 220% primary snippet is low, the mechanic reflects modern design: complex conditions for high rewards. He is stat-heavy enough to survive in the current 4-star meta.

In 4-star tournaments (especially Bloody Battle and Rush), he is a menace. In 5-star wars, he might struggle unless heavily protected, but his unique mechanic allows him to punch above his weight class if the battle goes long.
As a Rogue, Talésie has access to the Evade talent, which is arguably the best defensive talent in the game. However, his role is damage.
Maximizes the damage of his special skill and the Bleed DoT. Rogue nodes often have high attack values.
Slightly squishier, relying on Evade to survive.
Keeps him alive longer to let the Blood Lily stacks accumulate.

is recommended. Talésie is a damage dealer. His primary utility is killing things before they kill you. The Rogue evade chance handles the survival aspect well enough; you want that special skill to hit as hard as possible when it triggers on marked enemies.
He becomes more dangerous the longer the match lasts.
Can fire repeatedly, capitalizing on new marks.
The Family Bonus applies the marks automatically; he doesn't need to hit them first to mark them.
That dodge chance can win games.
Without Blood Lily marks, he is a very weak sniper (220% is laughable in the modern meta).
You can't control who gets marked (it's always highest HP), so you can't easily target a specific threat for the extra damage unless you manipulate HP bars carefully.
If the enemy can cleanse or block the marks (though marks are usually stacks, which are harder to remove), his effectiveness drops.
Note: Blood Lily is a Stack, so standard cleanse does not remove it.
The DoT and fast damage are king here. The healing from the family bonus (stealing health) is also a cheeky way to get health in a mode with no healing.
He becomes Very Fast, and the marks accumulate every turn regardless. He can spam his special.
Great for boss waves where the boss has high HP and accumulates stacks, taking massive damage.
The key to Talésie is patience and family synergy.

Running multiple Talésies (or pairing with the 3-star Eumachius if allowed/viable) accelerates the application of marks. With three family members, three enemies get marked every turn. By turn 3, almost the entire enemy team is marked, turning every Talésie fire into a full AoE wipe.
Control the mana with Proteus, lower defense with Tiburtus, and let Talésie clean up with direct damage and bleed on the marked targets.
Can sometimes absorb the damage meant for heroes.
Very rare, but heroes that prevent status effects or stacks specifically would neuter him.
Since he relies on turns passing to apply marks, a team that kills him in 2 turns prevents him from ramping up.

Talésie is a fantastic pull. He hits hard, survives with Rogue talent, and offers a fun mechanic. Focus on leveling him and look for other Sanguine heroes to pair him with, even the 3-star Yumakius, to boost the mark generation.

He is a staple for 4-star tournaments. If you care about performing well in weekly tourneys, he is worth the limit break. In 5-star warfare, he is likely too slow to ramp up compared to the devastating 5-star heroes available, but he is a fun toy for clean-up teams.
The community reaction to the Sanguine heroes has been mixed regarding the mechanics.

"This is really forcing players to either dig deep for a complete set or skip entirely. These are not synergies, this is one hero broken into two pieces." - A beta tester's concern about the reliance on the Blood Lily mark.
"I would definitely level Tassie up... looks really cool, like a bat humanoid thing." - A content creator's first impression.
Many players find the names "tongue twisters," with Talésie being one of the easier ones to pronounce compared to his 5-star cousins.

Talésie is a "slow burn" hero running at "fast" mana speed. It's a contradiction that works surprisingly well. He rewards you for surviving the first few turns by granting you the power to unleash devastating AoE attacks later in the match. While he relies heavily on his family mechanic to shine, the fact that the mechanic is automatic (start of turn) makes him user-friendly. He isn't just a bat out of hell; he's the bat that brings the hell with him.
A top-tier contender for 4-star tournaments and a must-level for anyone lucky enough to pull him.
A comprehensive review of Talésie, the 4-star Dark Rogue from the Sanguine Feast event. We analyze stats, the Blood Lily mechanic, emblem paths, and whether this bat-like assassin is worth your mats.
